decks.name: Helen Keller's Story
decks.description: Learn about the inspiring life of Helen Keller

cards.back: Helen Keller was an American author and speaker who was both deaf and blind. She became the first deaf-blind person to earn a college degree!

cards.front: Why couldn't Helen Keller see or hear?
cards.back: Helen Keller lost her ability to see and hear when she was only 19 months old due to an illness (possibly scarlet fever or meningitis).
hints: She became ill as a baby

cards.back: Anne Sullivan was Helen Keller's teacher and lifelong companion. She taught Helen to communicate by spelling words into her palm.

cards.front: What was the first word Helen understood?
cards.back: The first word Helen understood was 'water' (W-A-T-E-R). Anne spelled it into Helen's hand while water flowed over her other hand.

cards.front: What did Helen Keller achieve despite her disabilities?
cards.back: Helen graduated from college, wrote 12 books, gave speeches around the world, and inspired millions of people to overcome challenges.
An adjective is a describing word. It tells us more about a noun - like its color, size, shape, or quality. Examples: big, beautiful, brave, dark
'Tall' is the adjective. It describes the tree - telling us about its height.
Helen Keller was: brave, determined, hardworking, intelligent, inspiring, and courageous.

cards.front: Can we use more than one adjective for a noun?
cards.back: Yes! We can use multiple adjectives. Example: 'a beautiful, old, wooden box' uses three adjectives.

cards.front: Where do adjectives usually come in a sentence?
cards.back: Adjectives usually come before the noun they describe. Example: 'brave girl' (brave comes before girl).

decks.name: Courage & Inspiration
decks.description: Lessons about bravery from the chapter

cards.back: Courage means being brave - facing your fears and doing something difficult even when you're scared.

cards.front: Why was Helen Keller considered brave?
cards.back: Helen was brave because she never gave up despite not being able to see or hear. She worked hard to learn and achieved great things.

cards.front: What lesson does Helen Keller's story teach us?
cards.back: Helen's story teaches us that nothing is impossible with hard work and determination. We should never give up on our dreams!

cards.front: What does the poem 'Don't Be Afraid of the Dark' teach?
cards.back: The poem teaches us to be brave and not fear the darkness. Darkness is natural, and we shouldn't be scared of it.
